title: bothriochloa meridionalis
intro: Bothriochloa meridionalis, also known as the southern bluestem, is a species of grass native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which are said to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Bothriochloa meridionalis include its potential to aid in the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. In herbal preparations, the roots of Bothriochloa meridionalis are typically dried and used in decoctions or infusions, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
